title: Academic Timetable Scheduling Engine
problem: University timetables were created manually — managing 40+ sections, hundreds of course assignments, room constraints, and faculty load limits with no automatic conflict detection.
solution: Built a constraint-based scheduling engine that automatically assigns courses to time slots and rooms while enforcing faculty load limits, room capacity rules, section conflicts, and institutional break windows.
architecture: React Admin Dashboard → Spring Boot REST API → Constraint Scheduling Engine → PostgreSQL (Flyway migrations)
impact: Automates timetable generation in under 2 minutes with guaranteed conflict-free schedules and Excel export for departments.
role: Designed the domain model, implemented the scheduling algorithm, built the REST API, and developed the admin dashboard.

title: Offline AI Assistant Shell
problem: Developers rely on online AI assistants, but integrating AI directly into system environments introduces major security risks such as privilege escalation, unsafe system calls, and data exposure.
solution: Built a custom C-based shell with an embedded offline LLM assistant using llama.cpp, designed with a security-first architecture including syscall filtering, namespace isolation, and resource limits.
architecture: Custom C Shell → Sandboxed AI Worker → llama.cpp Inference Engine → TinyLlama Local Model
impact: Enables private, offline AI assistance directly inside the terminal while enforcing strict syscall filtering and process isolation.
role: Designed the shell architecture, implemented sandboxed AI worker execution, integrated local LLM inference, and engineered syscall filtering with Seccomp.

title: Air Notepad — Gesture-Based Drawing Interface
problem: Traditional input devices limit natural human-computer interaction. This project explores how computer vision can convert hand movements into structured drawing commands in real time.
solution: Built a gesture-controlled drawing interface using MediaPipe hand landmark detection. Raw hand keypoints are processed with custom gesture recognition and smoothing algorithms to convert finger motion into stable drawing strokes.
architecture: Webcam → MediaPipe Hand Landmarks → Gesture Recognition → Coordinate Smoothing → OpenCV Canvas Rendering
impact: Demonstrates real-time ML inference, landmark processing, gesture classification, and signal smoothing for interactive computer vision systems.
role: Designed gesture recognition logic, implemented smoothing algorithms to stabilize drawing strokes, and built the real-time computer vision processing pipeline.

title: MoodHarmonics — Multi-Model AI Music Generation System
problem: AI media generation often requires multiple specialized models for text, audio, and classification. Coordinating these models while managing heavy inference workloads and reliability is a systems challenge.
solution: Built an end-to-end ML system that converts text prompts into generated music and lyrics while automatically classifying genre using audio embeddings.
architecture: React UI → Flask API → Model Orchestration Layer → (MusicGen | GPT-based Lyrics | YAMNet Classification) → MongoDB + Local Storage
impact: Demonstrates multi-model ML orchestration, inference optimization via model preloading, and fault-tolerant API design with fallback inference paths.
role: Designed the ML orchestration pipeline, implemented model preloading to reduce inference latency, and built API layers to expose AI capabilities to the frontend.

title: Distributed URL Shortener (System Design Study)
problem: URL shortening services must handle massive traffic while generating collision-free short codes, preventing abuse, and scaling across multiple backend instances.
solution: Built a full-stack URL shortener implementing Base62 encoding for compact ID generation, sliding-window rate limiting to prevent abuse, and a round-robin load balancing simulation for multi-instance deployments.
architecture: React SPA → Spring Boot API → Rate Limiter Interceptor → URL Service → SQLite (JPA) with Base62 encoding
impact: Demonstrates core distributed systems patterns including ID encoding, request throttling, and horizontal scaling strategies. Designed with a roadmap for Redis caching, distributed ID generation, and horizontal scaling.
role: Designed the URL encoding system, implemented sliding-window rate limiting, and simulated multi-instance load balancing.

title: SootheFoot — Smart Insole Companion Application
problem: Wearable health devices generate sensor data that must be translated into meaningful user feedback and control interfaces.
solution: Developed a Flutter-based companion application to visualize foot pressure data and manage adaptive massage control for a prototype smart insole system.
architecture: Flutter Mobile App → Sensor Data Simulation API → User Profile Manager → SAP HANA Cloud (planned)
impact: Demonstrates IoT companion app design, sensor data visualization, and multi-user wearable device interaction.
role: Designed and implemented the mobile application interface, including sensor data visualization, multi-user profile management, and adaptive massage control logic.
